input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan:

---

## .NET MAUI Dev Environment Setup — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

### 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

### 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

### 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 (recommended) or Build Tools

If using full VS:
```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
```
Then modify the installation to include:
- **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload
- **Universal Windows Platform development** workload (for WinUI)

If headless / CLI-only, install Build Tools:
```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

### 4. Install the Android SDK (via CLI)

```powershell
# Install JDK (Android requires 17+)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Install Android command-line tools
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only
# Or let MAUI handle it — the workload auto-provisions under:
#   $env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk

# Discover what MAUI provisioned automatically:
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

### 5. Discover & Install Correct Android SDK Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# Set SDK path
$ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
$sdkmanager = "$ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List all available packages (find latest platform/build-tools)
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "platforms;android-" 
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "build-tools;"

# Install what you need (example — pick versions from the list above)
& $sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses
```

### 6. Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdmanager = "$ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdmanager list device 2>$null | Select-String "id:"

# Create an AVD (adjust system image to match step 5)
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_7"

# Launch it
& "$ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

### 7. Set Environment Variables (persistent)

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-Command java | Split-Path | Split-Path), "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH for adb
$currentPath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
$newEntry = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\platform-tools"
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$newEntry*") {
  [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",
    "$currentPath;$newEntry", "User")
}
```

### 8. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for WinUI target)

```powershell
# Run as Administrator
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

### 9. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 -t:Run
```

### 10. Troubleshoot / Verify

```powershell
# Check all workloads are healthy
dotnet workload repair

# Check what TFMs your project targets
dotnet --info

# Confirm Android SDK components
& $sdkmanager --list_installed
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Steps 2 & 4 overlap — `dotnet workload install maui` auto-provisions most Android SDK components. Step 5 is for fine-tuning or updating.
- The `net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0` TFM targets Windows 10 2004+. Adjust if you need a different minimum version.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for fast Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
